title: phyllostegia bracteata
intro: Phyllostegia bracteata, also known as Hawaiian lilac or ma'o, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its fragrant purple flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Phyllostegia bracteata are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Phyllostegia bracteata are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
